sector: Utilities — Independent Power Producer (IPP) / merchant power + retail
bull_thesis: Vistra is the cheapest way to own the physical power that every AI-capex dollar must ultimately pay for. It owns ~41 GW of dispatchable + carbon-free nuclear capacity sited in the two highest-load-growth US markets (ERCOT 5-6%/yr through 2030, PJM 2-3%), an asset base hyperscalers cannot replicate — siting, interconnection, and an operating nuclear license are multi-year, near-unbuildable barriers. The AI narrative is now CONTRACTED, not speculative: a 20-year Meta PPA for ~2,609 MW at the PJM nuclear plants and a 20-year AWS PPA for ~1,200 MW at Comanche Peak convert merchant optionality into 2042-dated investment-grade cash flow. Q1 2026 corroborated the trajectory — Ongoing Adj. EBITDA $1,494M (+20% YoY), revenue $5.64B (+43%), East EBITDA +56%. Guidance ($6.8-7.6B 2026, $7.4-7.8B 2027) DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES the $4.7B Cogentrix gas acquisition (H2-26 close, +~5,500 MW), the Lotus deal (closed, +2,600 MW), and the Meta/AWS PPA economics — a sandbag that resolves to upside. Capital return is relentless and disciplined: >$10B cash generation planned, >=$1.3B/yr returned, fresh $1B buyback authorization, share count already down ~30%+ since 2021 and compounding. Investment-grade upgrade at a 2nd agency. The whole edifice trades at ~10x forward EV/EBITDA vs Constellation (CEG) at ~13x and ~23x forward P/E — a 3-turn discount the market is slowly removing as VST re-rates out of the cyclical-merchant bucket toward the contracted-nuclear bucket. As contracted MW grows, ROIC and margins are RISING, not eroding — a widening moat the multiple has not yet caught.
bear_thesis: Much of what you are paying ~$55B + ~$19B net debt for is transient ERCOT scarcity-pricing dressed up as durable AI demand. The contracted PPAs (Meta + AWS ~3,800 MW) are real but are a MINORITY of a ~41 GW fleet — the majority of trailing EBITDA is merchant Texas power that mean-reverts hard if weather/scarcity normalize, and 2024-25 ERCOT prices were a high-water mark. The GAAP profit headline is ~70% non-cash hedge mark-to-market ($723M of the $1,029M Q1 net income), flattering the cash reality. Leverage is high and RISING — total debt jumped ~$17.0B to ~$19.2B in a single quarter on Cogentrix/Lotus M&A, against a thin ~$5.6B equity base (debt/equity ~330%); a power-price air-pocket or a credit-spread widening pressures both the buyback and the rating. The thesis shares its single load-bearing macro factor — AI-capex / data-center power durability — with the ENTIRE scale-out basket, so it offers no resolution-driver independence: a hyperscaler capex guide-down de-grosses VST alongside everything else. And the stock has ALREADY run — market cap +22% YoY, the cheap-merchant-IPP re-rate is largely spent; the easy multiple gap to CEG closes slowly and is partly justified by CEG's larger fully-contracted nuclear base. The 'sandbag' upside (Cogentrix, PPAs) is also partly priced — analysts already model it. Downside in a power-price normalization + AI-pause scenario is a re-rate back toward 7-8x merchant multiples.
sop_bull: $200-215/share. Whole-company intrinsic via EV/EBITDA on a contracted-fleet re-rate. 2027 guided Adj. EBITDA midpoint ~$7.6B, PLUS Cogentrix (~$0.5-0.7B EBITDA) + Meta/AWS PPA ramp (~$0.4-0.6B incremental) = ~$8.5-8.8B run-rate EBITDA not yet in guidance. Apply 12.0x (a half-turn below CEG's ~13x as the contracted mix rises) = ~$103B EV; less ~$20B net debt = ~$83B equity / ~335M sh = ~$215/sh on the high case (~$200 at 11.5x). Capital-return tailwind (share count compounding down >=$1.3B/yr) is additive over the holding period.
sop_bear: $110-125/share. Merchant-normalization case. Haircut EBITDA to ~$6.3-6.6B (ERCOT scarcity reverts, no PPA premium credited beyond contracted base) and apply an 8.5x merchant-IPP multiple = ~$56B EV; less ~$20B net debt = ~$36B equity / ~335M sh = ~$108/sh; ~$125 if you credit a 9.0x multiple and the contracted floor. This is the 'AI-pause + power-price air-pocket' anchor.
sop_breakdown: Base case: ~$7.8-8.0B forward run-rate Ongoing Adj. EBITDA (2027 guided midpoint ~$7.6B + partial credit for Cogentrix/Lotus integration and the contracted PPA ramp, conservatively NOT crediting the full sandbag) x ~10.75x blended EV/EBITDA (a discount to CEG's ~13x reflecting the still-merchant-heavy mix and higher leverage, but a premium to the 8.5x pure-merchant multiple as ~3,800 MW of 20-year PPAs contract the base) = ~$84-86B EV; less ~$20B net debt = ~$64-66B equity; / ~335M diluted shares = ~$165/share. The buyback (share count -1.5-2%/yr) and the explicit guidance sandbag (Cogentrix + Meta/AWS upside) provide the bridge from the current ~$164 toward and above fair value over 12-18 months; conversely the merchant tail caps how far the multiple can rerate without further contracting.
risk_reward: Roughly balanced-to-slightly-favorable but NOT asymmetric. Current ~$164. Downside-to-break (bear SoP) ~$110-125 = ~25-33% downside. Upside-to-fair (~$165 base) ~ flat; upside-to-bull (~$200-215) = ~22-31%. So ~1:1 reward/risk on the spread between bear-break and bull, with the base case essentially AT the current price — the cheap-merchant re-rate is largely captured. The favorable tilt comes from the guidance sandbag (Cogentrix + PPAs) being convertible into hard prints, not from a depressed entry.
peer_comps_note: TRUE peers are the AI-power / nuclear-merchant IPP cohort: Constellation Energy (CEG), Talen Energy (TLN), and NRG Energy (NRG). LIVE multiples verified 2026-06-22: CEG ~$88.7B market cap, ~23.6x forward P/E, ~13x forward EV/EBITDA (largest US power producer post-$29.1B Calpine close Jan-2026, fully-contracted nuclear; the premium comp). VST ~$55.2B market cap, ~14.7x forward NTM P/E, ~10x forward EV/EBITDA — the VALUE name of the cohort: cheapest on EV/EBITDA and P/E, with 2026 EBITDA $6.8-7.6B / 2027 $7.4-7.8B. TLN (Talen) ~11.5x EV/EBITDA on 2026 guide ($1.75-2.05B EBITDA, +43% growth) — highest-beta, smaller (~13 GW), Susquehanna nuclear + ~1,920 MW Amazon contract; the merchant cohort de-rated toward ~8x on pullbacks. RANKING WITHIN THE LAYER: VALUATION — VST cheapest (10x) < TLN (11.5x) < CEG (13x). GROWTH — TLN fastest (+43% EBITDA) > VST/CEG (mid-teens-to-20%, boosted by M&A). MARGINS — CEG highest-quality (fully-contracted nuclear), VST mid (merchant+contracted mix), TLN most volatile (merchant-heavy). RETURNS/scale — CEG > VST > TLN. AI-CAPEX EXPOSURE — all three are direct beneficiaries; CEG (nuclear-pure) and TLN (Susquehanna co-lo) are the 'cleanest' nuclear plays, VST is the diversified ERCOT+PJM play with the most dispatchable gas optionality (Cogentrix). VST is the discounted, more-diversified, more-levered member of a cohort that has ALL run hard on the same AI-power factor.
role_in_scaleout: VST is a Tier-1 POWER-GEN supplier — it owns the actual electrons (gas + carbon-free nuclear, ~41 GW) in ERCOT and PJM that hyperscaler/sovereign-AI compute must buy before any chip, optic, or transformer matters; demand-durability is HIGH for the contracted ~3,800 MW (20-year Meta/AWS PPAs) and merely cyclical for the merchant remainder, with the structural ERCOT/PJM load-growth (5-6% / 2-3% through 2030) underwriting the floor.
consensus_delta: The street's residual error is the LEGACY one — pricing VST as a cyclical, over-levered Texas merchant IPP that deserves an 8-9x merchant multiple and whose ERCOT EBITDA mean-reverts. That mispricing is ~60% closed: the stock is +22% YoY and now trades ~10x, no longer 7x. What the street STILL underweights: (1) guidance explicitly excludes Cogentrix (~5,500 MW, $4.7B), the closed Lotus deal, AND the Meta/AWS PPA economics — so consensus 2026/2027 EBITDA understates the contracted run-rate, a sandbag that prints as upside; (2) the contracted-MW mix is rising, which should pull the multiple toward CEG's 13x, not justify the 3-turn discount persisting. But the OPPOSITE consensus error also exists and partially offsets: many holders now price VST as a pure secular AI-power compounder and under-discount the still-majority merchant EBITDA that is genuinely price-sensitive. Net: the easy expectations-arb (deep-merchant-discount) is largely spent; the residual edge is the guidance sandbag converting to hard prints, which is real but modest and partly modeled.
valley_of_death: Three 3-9 month risks. (1) ERCOT/PJM power-price air-pocket: a mild-weather summer or scarcity-pricing normalization would compress merchant EBITDA and puncture the 'AI demand = durable' framing well before the PPAs ramp — the single biggest near-term de-rate risk. (2) AI-capex scare (the basket-wide tripwire in CATALYST_WATCH_REGIME.md §5): AI-capex is ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth and the dominant shared factor under the tape; any hyperscaler capex guide-down de-grosses the ENTIRE power-gen basket simultaneously, and VST has zero resolution-driver independence from CEG/TLN here. (3) Balance-sheet/financing: debt rose ~$17.0B to ~$19.2B in one quarter on M&A; the $4.7B Cogentrix close (H2-26) adds more; in a higher-for-longer/credit-spread-widening regime (Fed on hold to ~Dec per the regime doc, HY ~285bp) refinancing and the buyback compete for cash. None is a cash-burn hump — VST generates >60% FCF conversion — but a power-price + AI-pause combination is a real ~25-33% drawdown path to the bear SoP.
positioning_washout: Limited mechanical-selling risk. VST is not a dividend/yield-anchored holder base (the dividend is small; the story is buyback + growth), so there is no yield-investor liquidation trigger. The risk is the inverse: VST has become a crowded momentum/AI-power long across generalist and thematic-AI funds. A de-grossing event (AI-capex scare, power-price miss) would see fast-money trim the whole power-gen cohort (CEG/VST/TLN) together — a correlated unwind, not a clean idiosyncratic washout. There is no forced-seller catalyst that sets up an asymmetric entry; the better entry comes from a fundamental flush (a weak ERCOT-price quarter or an AI-capex headline), which is exactly the 'wait for capitulation' setup.
forcing_function: No single hard binary; a sequence of soft-extended milestones. (1) Cogentrix close (H2 2026) — converts the excluded ~$0.5-0.7B EBITDA into guidance, forcing an estimate raise. (2) Q2/Q3 2026 prints (Aug/Nov 2026) — the test of whether ERCOT summer scarcity-pricing holds the merchant EBITDA AND the first quarters to credit PPA ramp; these are the real re-rate/de-rate events. (3) Meta/AWS PPA revenue commencement and any NEW hyperscaler PPA announcement — each contracted-MW print pulls the multiple toward CEG. None is a dated binding catalyst with capped asymmetry; they are serially-extended operational milestones — hence SOFT_EXTENDED.
moderator_conclusion: VST is the highest-quality, cheapest-multiple member of the POWER-GEN cohort and a genuine Tier-1 supplier of the one input — physical electrons — that gates the entire AI build-out. The Interrogator's read (STRENGTHENING, NARROW-but-WIDENING moat, manageable secular threat) is well-corroborated: +20% EBITDA, real 20-year Meta/AWS contracts, IG upgrade, disciplined buyback, and a guidance sandbag that excludes its two biggest growth drivers. The forensic quality is high (score 4). BUT the regime discipline forces a down-rate from what the fundamentals alone would earn. Applying CATALYST_WATCH_REGIME.md: this is NOT a hard-dated catalyst inside a favorable window — it is a set of SOFT_EXTENDED operational milestones (Cogentrix close, quarterly ERCOT prints, PPA ramps), and the load-bearing catalyst (the AI-power-demand re-rate) has ALREADY substantially FIRED (+22% YoY, multiple expanded from ~7x to ~10x). §4's explicit instruction to PENALIZE already-fired / already-ran catalysts and to PRIZE resolution-driver independence both cut against VST: its entire thesis hinges on the SAME single shared macro factor — AI-capex durability — as the rest of the basket, with zero independence, and AI-capex is named as the dominant under-the-tape risk (§5 tripwire). The base-case SoP (~$165) sits essentially AT the ~$164 price: most of the cheap-merchant re-rate is captured, the asymmetry is ~1:1, and the residual edge (guidance sandbag) is real but modest and partly modeled. This is a WATCHLIST-FOR-CAPITULATION (B), conviction 3: own the quality, but wait for a specific flush — a weak ERCOT-price quarter or an AI-capex headline that de-grosses the cohort — to get a non-priced-in entry toward the bear-SoP zone ($120-140). value_conviction is ALSO 3: stripping the catalyst/regime overlay, the SoP fair value (~$165) is roughly fair-to-slightly-rich vs the ~$164 price, the moat is widening (so no value-trap cap applies), and the forensic quality is high — a fairly-priced quality compounder, not a margin-of-safety bargain. role_in_scaleout: VST sells the electrons every AI dollar must buy; demand is durable for the contracted ~3,800 MW and cyclical for the merchant remainder.
interrogator_dossier:
# Vistra Corp. (VST) — Interrogator Forensic Dossier

*Source basis: VST Q1 2026 earnings release (8-K, 2026-05-07), Q1 2026 earnings-call transcript, Q3/Q4 2025 results, 10-Q FY2026, and live market data as of 2026-06-22.*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC
Vistra entered this window as a deeply-discounted, over-levered Texas merchant IPP whose story was 'FCF + buybacks shrink the count faster than the market notices.' That story has EXPANDED and re-rated into a national AI-power-demand growth narrative without abandoning the capital-returns spine. 2024->early-2025: relentless buybacks (count down ~30% since 2021), Energy Harbor nuclear (2024) bolting ~6.4 GW carbon-free onto a Texas-gas fleet, 'comprehensive hedging locks 2025-26 EBITDA.' Mid-2025->now: pivot to STRUCTURAL load growth — '5-6% ERCOT through 2030, 2-3% PJM,' framed as 'not speculative' but visible in 'interconnection queues and physical load connections.' New pillars: Meta 20-yr PPA ~2,609 MW (PJM nuclear); AWS 20-yr PPA ~1,200 MW (Comanche Peak, TX); pending $4.7B Cogentrix (~5,500 MW gas, H2-26); closed Lotus (~2,600 MW, Nov-25); ~4,500 MW organic gas. The buyback emphasis shifted from 'shrink the count' to 'fund growth AND return >=$1.3B/yr.' A genuine thesis EXPANSION (merchant-cyclical -> contracted-AI-growth), corroborated by hard contracts.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS
'~20% EBITDA growth, structural demand' -> Q1-26 Ongoing Adj EBITDA $1,494M vs ~$1,245M PY (+20%); revenue $5.64B vs $3.93B (+43%); East EBITDA +56%, Texas +19% — CORROBORATED. 'Swing to profitability' -> GAAP NI +$1,029M vs $(317)M, but $723M is unrealized hedge MTM — HALF-TRUE/FLAGGED (~70% of swing non-cash). '2026 EBITDA $6.8-7.6B; 2027 $7.4-7.8B' -> Q1 run-rate supports midpoint; guidance EXCLUDES Cogentrix + Meta/AWS PPAs — CREDIBLE + sandbagged. '>$10B cash gen, >=$1.3B/yr returned' -> YTD ~$525M buybacks + ~$600M total returns in 4 mo — CORROBORATED. 'IG balance sheet' -> upgraded at 2nd agency, BUT debt ~$17.0B->$19.2B in one quarter, equity only ~$5.6B (D/E ~330%); net leverage ~2.5-2.7x EBITDA is IG-comfortable for a contracted IPP — MIXED, watch the trend.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY
RISING. 2025->Q1-26 language migrated from defensive/hedging ('locked in,' 'comprehensive hedge') to offensive/demand ('structural load growth,' 'not speculative,' 'interconnection queues'). Volunteers multi-year load numbers with specificity; raised 2027 EBITDA opportunity; fresh $1B buyback authorization. No shift to cost/efficiency defense.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY
HIGH. Multi-year record of meeting/beating EBITDA guidance and over-delivering buybacks (count -30%+ since 2021). Q4-25 soft print dinged the stock, but Q1-26 rebound (+20%) re-validated and 2026 guidance was REAFFIRMED. Excluding Cogentrix + PPAs is a sandbag that historically resolves to upside. Reliability: High (one Q4-25 wobble).

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS
Signed-vs-queue load growth (gap between headline load and CONTRACTED MW); leverage/M&A appetite at ~$19B debt; ERCOT power-price normalization (how much EBITDA is transient scarcity vs durable) — management deflects toward the structural-demand frame rather than modeling price mean-reversion.

## 6. RED/GREEN FLAGS
GREEN: real dated 20-yr contracts (Meta ~2,609 MW, AWS ~1,200 MW) convert narrative to bankable cash flow; capital-return discipline through the re-rate; IG upgrade at 2nd agency; guidance excludes biggest drivers (sandbag). RED: ~70% of GAAP swing is non-cash hedge MTM; absolute leverage rising ($17->$19.2B) on thin ~$5.6B equity; ERCOT scarcity tailwind partly cyclical; thesis shares its single macro factor (AI-capex) with the whole basket — correlation risk.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS
Terminology shift 'merchant/hedged' -> 'contracted/carbon-free/structural' — deliberately re-rating out of the cyclical-IPP bucket toward the CEG bucket (3x multiple). Capital language widened 'buyback' -> 'fund growth AND return.' Meta deal at PJM (not TX) consciously diversifies AI-demand exposure geographically.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT
STRENGTHENING. Single most important thing to weigh: how much EBITDA is durable contracted cash flow vs transient ERCOT scarcity — the re-rate assumes the AI premium is permanent, but a chunk of trailing EBITDA is merchant TX power that mean-reverts; contracted PPAs (~3,800 MW) de-risk the forward base but are not yet the majority. MOAT: scale-cost + regulatory-license + asset-scarcity — ~41 GW dispatchable + nuclear in ERCOT/PJM that hyperscalers cannot replicate (siting, interconnection, nuclear license = multi-year barriers); NARROW-to-WIDE and WIDENING (rising returns/margins by the evidence test), capped at NARROW by the merchant tail + leverage. SECULAR: the dominant vector is a TAILWIND (electrification + AI-power = energy-transition winner); only risks are an AI-capex stall (manageable — load growth also rests on electrification/reindustrialization) and power-price normalization (cyclical, not secular). Cheapness vs CEG is a fear/skepticism print, not a shrinking base.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: WIDENING | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
skeptic_kill_fact: No kill_fact found. Every load-bearing figure verifies against primary sources: Q1 2026 Ongoing Adj. EBITDA $1,494M and revenue $5.64B (8-K, 2026-05-07); the $17,043M->$19,163M debt jump and $5,610M equity / ~330% D/E (10-Q, FY2026); the pending $4.7B / 5,500 MW Cogentrix close H2-26 explicitly excluded from reaffirmed 2026 guidance ($6.8-7.6B); and the Meta ~2,609 MW + AWS 1,200 MW 20-year nuclear PPAs (SEC 8-Ks). The bear's own attack vectors (merchant tail, leverage, basket-correlated single-factor AI-capex risk) are real but are already priced INTO the B/conviction-3 down-rate, not disqualifiers.
skeptic_corrections: STALE/WRONG ANCHOR on the price-momentum claim: the bull thesis and Interrogator dossier assert VST is '+22% YoY' and that the AI re-rate 'has ALREADY substantially FIRED (+22% YoY, multiple expanded ~7x->10x)' and that 'market cap +22% YoY.' This is FALSE per live market data: VST is DOWN ~7.5% over the trailing year, trading $163.75 vs a 52-week HIGH of $219.82 (~25% off the high). The error cuts FOR the thesis, not against it: the central moderator objection ('the name has already run, the re-rate is spent, asymmetry is ~1:1') is materially weaker than stated -- the stock has in fact de-rated ~25% off its highs, which is closer to the 'wait-for-capitulation flush' the moderator says to wait for. The 'priced-in / already-ran' demotion rests partly on a wrong premise; the genuine entry is better than the dossier implies. Second minor correction: GAAP NI is cited as ~$1,029M with ~$723M (70%) non-cash hedge MTM -- the headline NI verifies (~$1.03B), and the ~70% non-cash characterization is directionally fair; the durable-vs-transient-EBITDA caveat stands.
skeptic_evidence: Vistra Q1 2026 8-K earnings release (filed 2026-05-07, SEC EDGAR 000169281926000011): Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA $1,494M, operating revenue $5.64B vs $3.93B PY; 2026 guidance reaffirmed $6.8-7.6B, explicitly excludes Cogentrix.

Vistra Q1 2026 10-Q (SEC EDGAR 000169281926000014, FY2026): long-term debt incl. current maturities $19,163M (up from $17,043M); total equity $5,610M (D/E ~330%); Lotus acquisition closed at $1,231M; $2.25B senior secured notes (Jan-2026) + $4.0B senior unsecured notes (Apr-2026) issued.

Vistra SEC 8-Ks / World Nuclear News (2025-2026): 20-year PPAs -- Meta ~2,609 MW across PJM nuclear (incl. 433 MW uprates), AWS 1,200 MW at Comanche Peak (delivery from Q4-2027, full ramp by 2032); ~3,800 MW contracted total.

Live market data (as of 2026-06-18): VST $163.75, market cap ~$55.2B, 52-week high $219.82, trailing-12-month return ~ -7.5% -- i.e. the stock is ~25% off its high and DOWN YoY, contradicting the dossier's '+22% YoY / re-rate already fired' claim.

scale.role: Cheapest way to own the dispatchable+nuclear power every AI dollar pays for; ~41 GW in the two highest-load-growth US markets, ~3,800 MW of 20-yr nuclear PPAs (Meta/AWS).
scale.rationale: The single best risk/reward in the whole 45-name complex and a CORE franchise (~86): firm dispatchable + nuclear under 20-yr hyperscaler PPAs in the highest-load-growth US markets, plus the Cogentrix optionality excluded from guidance. Skeptic verdict CONFIRMED_WITH_CORRECTIONS, and the correction cuts FOR the thesis — the bull's '+22% YoY / re-rate already fired' premise was FALSE; VST is actually DOWN ~7.5% YoY, ~25% off its high, i.e. the re-rate has NOT fired here — so only a ~4pt haircut. Base ~$165 ~at spot, bull +22-31%; the merchant tail and ~330% D/E are the offsets (in valuation_posture, not the conviction). This is the CORE I would seat at full weight today — enter_now_carry. [Honesty note: at ~$164 VST is FAIR to its own $160-170 SoP (+0.8%), NOT cheap on the absolute number — the 'cheap'/priority read is RELATIVE (cheapest of the power-gen cluster, ~26% off its 52wk high, below the 200-DMA), i.e. a drawdown-staging entry, not an absolute-SoP discount.]
scale.demand_durability: Very high — firm dispatchable + nuclear power under 20-yr hyperscaler PPAs in load-growth markets; merchant tail and ~330% D/E are the offsets.
scale.valuation_posture: fair vs SoP ($160-170, +0.8%); cheap only relative (-26% off 52wk high, <200-DMA)
